name: Basic Pasta Sauce

ingredients.item: can San Marzano tomatoes (I use whole peeled) plus one can of water

method: Chop onion, celery, and carrot into small pieces. In a frying pan on medium heat, saute vegetables until lightly browned in 2 T olive oil. Add the can of tomatoes (if you make fresh tomato sauce, use that). Break up tomatoes a little and then add a can of water, salt, and pepper. Tear four basil leaves, and place on top. Cover and simmer on low for 20 minutes or until the water has evaporated. In the meantime, boil salted water and add 300 grams of spaghetti. Cook until al dente. See my Introduction to learn how to cook pasta al dente. Add pasta directly to the sauce. If the sauce is too thick you can add a ladle of pasta water or two, mix, and then manticare. The sauce should cling to the pasta and be thick but not too thick, and not watery at all. Serves 2-3. Serving suggestion: fresh salad and homemade bread. Olé
